quotes.text: @5150Fishing Oh…2012-2016 timeframe. I’ve actually switched back to the ProV1x, since that post, because I bought a new launch monitor for my studio and I use RCT balls. I hate how hard the balls feel but love the performance and the fact they are all x-rayed. The Wilson Duo were always salt water tested before use because there would be 2–3 balls per dozen that were unusable🤷
quotes.author: @Skyking6976
quotes.source_title: Srixon Soft Feel VS Bridgestone E6 Soft VS Callaway Supersoft 2025 | Battle of the Softies

quotes.text: Still strange how the golf balls I like the most seem to do so poorly in Nick's evaluations. Love the Srixon Soft Feel.  It's the only one from Srixon Nick hated. Love the Wilson 50 Elite. Nick hated them. Heck, he even pulled strings and got Wilson to discontinue them.  Bummer. Now I do love the Bridgestone E6, so small win there. Nick did not hate them. Did not love them..... But did not hate them. I'm embarrassed to be me.
quotes.author: @toddc3988
quotes.source_title: Srixon Soft Feel VS Bridgestone E6 Soft VS Callaway Supersoft 2025 | Battle of the Softies

quotes.text: It is interesting seeing these balls compared when the pricing of these in Australia is so different. Bridgestones are $50 per dozen, supersofts are $45, and Srixons are $34. If I were going to choose some softer balls for winter, I don't think I could go past the Srixons at that price. Unless I am able to get the others on sale or something. Love the content though.
quotes.author: @Rhys_Something
quotes.source_title: Srixon Soft Feel VS Bridgestone E6 Soft VS Callaway Supersoft 2025 | Battle of the Softies

quotes.text: I don't use Prov1. I have tried Kirkland in the past and it wasn't that good compared to my gaming ball. Everyone compares to Prov1 but in reality it isn't the best anyway.

quotes.source_title: New Kirkland vs Pro V1 – Which One Should You REALLY Play?

quotes.text: I enjoyed the analysis, but I don't understand the choice to launch the 93 MPH  driver at such a low angle. By coincidence, this is my exact ball speed. I find that at this ball speed I get the best results from approximately a 17 degree launch. If I launched at 12.5 degrees, as you did, I would get the same results, about 210 carry, but my carry is closer to 220 at 17 degrees, assuming about 2500 backspin. I find I can go up to about 21 degrees before I start to lose total distance (assuming I can  keep the spin low).
quotes.author: @gregwroblewski4178
quotes.source_title: ROBOT GOLF BALLS TEST 2025 | The Best Golf Balls of the Year!

quotes.text: Basing it on swing speed and compression doesn't work for everyone as I use the ProV1X and my swing speed is 87mph and ball speed is 133 mph with an average of 200 metres (218 yards) for the driver playing of an 8 handicap. I hate the Supersoft and some similar balls as my game centers around the greens and they just don't spin on our greens so are impossible to stop especially of our firm fairways and around the greems when you need to stop a ball quicker,

quotes.text: Found my new gamer!!  2025 Wilson Triad.  I don't care what else is out there.  That ball feels great, very forgiving  (not like the 2023 version) long, strsight, stops on the green and loves to roll off the putter.  (Putting has been my learning curve as that ball rolls straighter and further, but on my 3rd round it's getting much better.  My first round putting 😂😂😂😂  yeah, lots of 3 putts from blowing by much further than what I am used too.) 2025 Wilson Triad was the most accurate ball they tested in 2024 on that massive robot golf ball test.  Fery "far-giving"

quotes.text: That is so not true. I max out at 135 ball speed(90 swing speed)with driver and like you state in this video I bought into lower compression is needed for my ball. Well, after recently purchasing a gc3 I went on a mission to find my ball, I bought a sleeve of EVERY ball out there and I was absolutly dumbfounded when of all the balls out there the TP%x and PRov1x were hands down the best out there. quotes.text: Wilson Staff Triad is great. I was always a Pro V1 guy, but then I tried Vice Pro and Pro Plus and they were great preforming balls for be less money. I’ve always played Wilson Staff irons so this year I tried the Triad ball and it is great. I haven’t played a Pro V1 since. In my opinion Wilson Staff has always made some of the best irons in golf.  I’m so glad they are making a great ball now. Also the Dyno-power Driver and woods look to be good.

quotes.text: Good video guys thanks. I hunt for balls at my course, so get to hit just about every ball ever made. Im an 11 hcp 100 swing speed max with driver. My favourite ball is prov1 x v left dash, prov1 x. Love the wilson staff. Great allrounder. Dont find enough wilson staff x to try. But im sure they would be nice. Srixon diamond is my favourite iron ball. But lose a bit with driver. Xv nice arounder. The longest drive I've ever hit was with a z star down wind water on both sides of Fairway 325mtr. I cannot get chrome tour to stop on greens. Otherwise, i would use it. Dont mind the driver distance

quotes.text: I’m 72 and I read a lot of reviews including your fine video’s with objective comments and excellent advice. With everything I read I’m totally using the wrong compression golf balls. My swing speed with a driver floats around 79-83 mph and I basically play bogey golf. Over the last couple year’s I won my flight of our Club Championship, came in 2nd the next year, and shot the lowest round of my life this year, a 75! I saved the balls from those 3 achievements, in order they were a TM TP5x, Callaway Chrome Soft X, and another TM TP5x. And to be totally honest I used those golf balls not based

quotes.text: I bought 4 boxes of Kirkland balls this year. Still have 3 and a half dozen after summer. I’m a weekend golfer and that equals about 2 per weekend lost. My drives are right about 300 yards and my handicap dropped from a 13.7 to a 11.1. 24 balls. At 1.60ish a ball I’m very satisfied. I doubt they will be as good as this years next year but we shall see I suppose.

quotes.text: Hi Geoff nice look at the my Spy results👍, Why didn’t you list the slow swing Driver results 🤔, I’m a senior golfer with a Swing speed of 77mph play off 8hcp & 78year old, play ProV1x, the only other Balls in my testing that are as good for Distance and Control are Callaway Chrome Tour X, & Maxfly Tour X, when you have a slow swing speed you need height & Spin to get Max Distance (why can’t most tester’s see this🤔Thanks Dickey

quotes.text: I have had a good look at the article and yes I think they need to do more testing from more brands. I have tried a lot of balls in the last 2 years, I have played from Pro V1 X to Noodles, I prefer a 3 piece ball, 2 piece I find clicky off the putter and irons, 4 + piece balls are costly but they are great around the greens. I did try the taylormade tour response, if you like a high flight ball or you need a higher flight ball then it’s for you. I found it ballooning on me and I lost distance. I play Maxfli Tour now, good spin around the green soft off the putter, lower flight off the driver,

quotes.text: I have an 80-85 mph swing speed.   I used to use a Taylormade Distance Plus (compression 77).   I hit it well sometimes, but had trouble getting enough launch height on drives.   I switched to a Bridgestone e6 (compression 45) and now I launch it further and higher on a consistent basis.   Compression matters.

quotes.text: As a 2HCP player down 4 strokes in 2024, started back after covid since teenager in the early 1990s. I avoid urethane balls. Number 1 ball to lose. I can line up 3 Trusofts / Soft feels/ AD333s. Land within a blanket throw  of eacher.  Scores of 75 happen I repeat with ProV1, TP5, Chromesoft or Kirkland, i need 4-5 blankets at each club.  80% GIRs at 165-205 yards using 2 piece balls. 50%-65% 3 piece, they grab the face too much creating fades, slices and hooks.

quotes.text: I'm 67 and have been quite beat up over my lifetime by various sports.  I don't swing very fast anymore and have experimented with tons of different golf balls.  Recently I played 3 rounds with high compression balls as a test.  Mostly Pro v1s that I find around my home course.  My usual ball is a Callaway Supersoft and I have consistent straight drives and consistent distance.  During my experiment, my drives were off center and quite erratic and with my irons the balls came up very short of my normal ball.  I satisfied myself that the more popular and expensive titelist was not for me.  Just

quotes.text: First off, thank you very much! But, having the data on the big screen like last year was amazing! Also, left to right dispersion is incredibly important data to test! With distances barely 5 yards difference with driver, dispersion is the more important decision. As of now, 2022 Triad and Mizuno Pro S are the two straightest balls I’ve played. Pro V1 is next, and I haven’t tried the Chrome Tour base model. Love the Srixion Diamond and will probably go back to them but the Staff Model X and Chrome Tour will need to be tried first.

quotes.text: Definitely need to bring back the shot dispersion area. I would rather sacrifice 4 yards longer to get a tighter dispersion. I mean on a drive if it’s 275 carry the area is 65 yards wide but the other ball is let’s say 271 but shot dispersion is only 18 yards wide. I’m taking the 18 every single time without hesitation.

quotes.text: Great video and data breakdown. Unlike others, I did enjoy the fact that you had a human swinging at the ones that had the best robot data. Robots don’t give feedback. I’ve recently been going through a few different balls looks for the best performance/value ratio for me. Used information from robot tests and human reviews both. The robot data tells you what the “perfect swing” can get. I am far from the perfect swing. Have tried Vice Tour and Pro, Maxfli Tour X, PXG Xtreme, Callaway ERC Soft, and Pro V1. Maxfli was longest off the driver at 275, but I couldn’t get a decent iron or wedge shot

quotes.text: Companies with very good quality control will see their balls fly true! Like Titleist, very consistent😊. Maxfli Tour (T X S) balls and are balance spun in the factory, their Straightfli uses dimple pattern for straight flight. Wilson Triad has a balanced material make up for that ball. Balls with a thick 360 line ( TM Tour Response) fly straight too due the heavier concentration of the paint. I use a Check Go Pro to spin mine!

quotes.text: Thanks for your transparency and honesty. Class act. It does raise an interesting question however. quotes.text: Disagree because very fast swing speed needs lower spin and launch. Funny enough at 125 mph driver and 105+ irons the 80 compression regular chromesoft works better.  The 99 compression balls will spin out if your mind at those speeds and usually fly 150+’ up.  At those heights control becomes difficult due to wind changes combined with excessive spin.  This is why Bryson is trying to redesign a golf ball for his swing currently

quotes.text: Switched from a yellow Pro-V1 to a yellow Wilson Triad based on this and other similar videos.  I'm very happy with the Triad, it's better for short game and I don't really notice any difference in the long game.  My driver swing speed is 93mph, i e. 80 compression is optimal.

quotes.text: In a 2024 compression video, you referenced a chart from Golf Sidekick that lists most (not all) golf balls and their compression.  Great resource.  Thank you! The chart recommends higher swing speeds than ball compression.  For example; the recommended driver swing speed for many balls with 70 compression is +80 MPH.  I imagine this is to ensure full compression without over-compressing the ball and thereby losing distance.  However, if a player has a +80 MPH swing speed, they could probably almost completely compress a ball with 90 compression and still get the added benefits of spin around

quotes.text: I play Titleist AVX and have a slow swing as a senior. I may drive the ball shorter than average, but my short game is better than average. I have of course tested low compression balls, but I need more spin around the greens to score. I prefer Avx or ProV1, but also like the new Wilson Triad. Forget about choosing golf ball from swing speed. I hit all three of the balls I mentioned longer than TM Speedsoft and other soft balls. ….and the shortest ball for me is…Callaway Supersoft. For me golf is more enjoyable with balls that I can control on and around the greens.

quotes.text: @rawkinlb73  I’ve had exactly the same experience, and I have the same swing speed as you. I am not a golf ball snob and in fact was really looking forward to switching to an inexpensive golf ball that was more “appropriate” for my swing. But over and over again, I found the ProV1s and Xs performed and felt better. Even with the Maxfli’s I found the Tour and Tour soft felt harder and clickier and I’ve settled on the Tour x which is the “wrong” ball for me. Feels great (softer than the “soft”) and I am able to hold greens. My takeaway is that this is a very personal feel/experience thing.

quotes.text: Compression matters a lot - but what matters as much for hitting irons/hybrids to greens and chipping is the cover. Soft greens can take a cheaper soft ball (Supersoft) and it will stop. But hit a Supersoft to very hard greens and it will bounce over the back. IMO the BEST ball for moderate swing speeds (80-90 mph with driver) is either Wilson Triad or Taylor Made Tour Response. Because they both have the right compression, and also have a Urethane cover that will grip on firmer greens.  And they putt better too - more consistent off the putter face.
